title: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python web app

messages.content: Write infrastructure config files. Be precise and correct. Use current best practices. Do not add unnecessary layers or features.

Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Python web application with these requirements:

- Stage 1 (builder): python:3.12-slim base, install build deps (gcc), copy requirements.txt, pip install into /install prefix
- Stage 2 (runtime): python:3.12-slim base, copy /install from builder, create non-root user 'appuser', copy app code, set WORKDIR /app, expose port 8000, run with CMD ["python", "-m", "uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
- Use a .dockerignore-friendly approach (COPY requirements.txt first)
- Minimize layers

Output the Dockerfile only, no explanation.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models often add unnecessary packages (vim, curl) or use single-stage builds that leave build deps in the runtime image.

title: Write a systemd service file with proper security hardening

messages.content: Write a correct systemd service file. Follow systemd best practices for security hardening. Do not add directives that conflict with each other.

Write a systemd service file (/etc/systemd/system/myapi.service) for a Python web API:

- Description: My API Service
- Start after network is available
- ExecStart: /opt/myapi/venv/bin/python -m uvicorn app.main:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8000
- WorkingDirectory: /opt/myapi
- Restart on failure with 5s delay
- Run as user 'myapi' group 'myapi'
- Security hardening: NoNewPrivileges, ProtectSystem=full, ProtectHome, PrivateTmp, ReadWritePaths=/opt/myapi/data
- WantedBy multi-user.target

Output the service file content only.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models frequently mix up Restart values (on-failure vs always), omit RequiredStartLimit, or write malformed directives.

title: Write an nginx reverse proxy with TLS and rate limiting

messages.content: Write a correct nginx configuration. Use proper nginx directive syntax. Do not mix up http/server/location context directives.

Write an nginx server block config file for a reverse proxy with these requirements:

- Listen on 443 with TLS (ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/example.com/fullchain.pem, ssl_certificate_key privkey.pem)
- Server name: example.com and www.example.com
- Rate limiting zone: 'api_limit' at 10r/s, burst 20, nodelay, applied to /api/ location
- Proxy pass to http://127.0.0.1:8000 with standard proxy headers (Host, X-Real-IP, X-Forwarded-For, X-Forwarded-Proto)
- Static file serving for /static/ from /var/www/static/ with expires 30d
- Redirect all HTTP (port 80) to HTTPS
- Client max body size: 10m

Output the complete nginx config (including the limit_req_zone declaration in the http context note, and the server blocks) — use a comment for the http-context directive since this is a server-block-include file.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models often put limit_req_zone in the server block (wrong context), omit X-Forwarded-Proto, or forget the HTTP-to-HTTPS redirect.

title: Write a deployment shell script with error handling and idempotency

messages.content: Write a robust bash script. Use set -euo pipefail. Handle errors. Make it idempotent. Quote variable expansions. Do not use deprecated syntax.

Write a bash deploy script (deploy.sh) that:

1. Uses set -euo pipefail
2. Defines APP_DIR=/opt/myapi and GIT_REPO variables
3. Has a log() function that prints timestamps to stderr
4. Creates APP_DIR if it doesn't exist (idempotent)
5. If a .git directory exists in APP_DIR, git pull; else git clone
6. Creates/recreates a Python venv at APP_DIR/venv
7. Installs requirements with pip
8. Restarts the systemd service 'myapi'
9. Checks service status and exits non-zero if it failed
10. Has a cleanup trap that removes the venv on failure

Output the script only with #!/usr/bin/env bash shebang.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Models often write set -e instead of set -euo pipefail, forget quoting, or skip the cleanup trap.
